title: Life after Evangelion 30 10
wikitext:
[]: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Duong » Wed Aug 09, 2023 5:49 am After Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0, Shinji and Gangs now live a normal live. What do you think their life will be?

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Wed Aug 09, 2023 4:13 pm Unless there was some fundamental alteration to real-life Japan, an impending climate crisis.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by nerv bae » Thu Aug 10, 2023 6:37 pm Unless there was some nonmetatextual transition to real-life Japan, the village's rice harvest.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Mackerel » Fri Aug 25, 2023 11:58 am probably very similar to the real world, so quite possibly worse than before

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Sat Aug 26, 2023 12:21 am Asuka becomes famous. It's inevitable.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by AdamMalkovitch » Tue Jul 30, 2024 8:26 pm Fanservice headcannon: Shinji and Mari go all-in with their relationship and have a ton of babies- Shinji frequently has Mari wear a purple wig and call him Shinji-kun when they're alone together but doesn't dare tell her why. He becomes a savant piano player. Adult Asuka starts dating Kensuke and they have one of those really mushy public display of affection relationships that makes everyone uncomfortable in public. The WUNDER crew work in Village-3 to begin modernizing it and building it into a proper city for the future generations. Pen-Pen becomes God. Realistically: Adult Shinji shares an awkward age-gap friendship with Mari where they both are clearly into each other but don't ever do anything about it. He's also got PTSD and spends a lot of time in the bombed-out remains of the EVA cage where Rei Q died. Adult Asuka knocks on Kensuke's train service hut door, finally ready to date him- only to discover he's gay. Most of the crew of the WUNDER is bored out of their minds in Village-3 working in the rice fields [motherfucker] and begin working with KREDIT- except Kitakimi, who surprisingly loves the manual labor and simple life. Pen-Pen becomes God [I refuse to elaborate.]

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Sep 25, 2024 8:09 pm The Suzuharas formally adopt Rei, who in turn adopts the kitten from earlier in the movie. Shinji gets to properly return her confession. Maybe he keeps living with Asuka & Kensuke because might as well. (at least assuming Misato is dead-dead) - it would be interesting to see him try to build a brotherly relationship with Misato's kid. When he eventually marries Rei he ends up being called 'Shinji Suzuhara'. (I would also accept him getting with Kaworu, as long as Rei still gets adopted by the Suzuharas. or all 3 being poly together) They live some chill, low-key calm & peaceful life without making any waves. Asuka might get with either Kensuke, Mari, both or neither of them, but either way it's very important to me that she doesn't change her mind about not wanting kids. After an extended self-care break, she probably eventually takes some fancy sounding big-shot job like aerospace engineer. Mari & Kaworu finally get to fulfill their dreams of becoming a sci-fi author and a make-up artist, respectively. Kodama & Nozomi return from being tanged to find their sister is a lot older than they remember her. The Reunion between Midori & her folks is likewise awkward. It's gonna be a mindfuck for the ppl who lived those "extra" 14 years of post apocalyptic hell but one imagines they eventually cope as ppl did with all sorts of freak historical events.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Sep 26, 2024 9:21 pm Kendrix wrote: it's very important to me that she doesn't change her mind about not wanting kids

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Sep 28, 2024 9:19 am BernardoCairo wrote: I would like to point ou that this was said only by the original Asuka, not Shikinami. Now, I'm not saying that Shikinami would want kids. But considering they're pretty much two different characters, who knows? I always thought that Asuka not wanting to have children had a lot to do with her mother. Of course, this is no longer an issue. There may be other factors now, though.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Oct 10, 2024 4:19 pm That's exactly why I hate the prospect of her having kids, because it comes with the idea that having kids must be the "default" and that not wanting them must be a sign of either damage or immaturity, that kids are a necessary part of a "happy" ending, or that growing up or 'becoming healthy' means to realize you want kids. Not wanting kids is a perfectly valid sentiment that doesn't need "fixing", and I find the suggestion of the opposite pretty offensive. It's the refrain of "you'll change your mind" that ppl who don't want kids always hear. Which doesn't reflect reality imho in my experience most ppl who love big families wanted them from a young age, often since the first time they babysitted a cousin or younger sibling, and ppl who were strongly against it often talk about how they never changed their mind and wished their relatives would shut up. If someone wrote a fanfic where Rei learns to appreciate the joys of steak or it's shown that she only avoided it because Gendo forbade her from eating it, you would definitely assume that the author thinks poorly of vegetarianism. Likewise, if the one character who explicitly says they don't want kids is constantly changed into wanting kids, to the point that it's hard to find any fanfiction where she doesn't get any bloody kids, that seems contrived at least. I don't understand why ppl can't just give the babies to... literally anyone who isn't Asuka or Ritsuko. Anno himself doesn't have kids. Maybe that's one of the points where he's putting a bit of himself into his characters. He didn't change his mind even after getting a wife. It would be one thing if Asuka was saying something like how she thinks she'd be a bad mother because of her own or how she thinks being a mother means something bad, but she doesn't say anything like that, she simply says she doesn't want kids to the point she doesn't even care to have the option. What about her remotely suggests that she would want kids? We don't see her gushing over kids, acting nurturing, caring or responsible, wishing she had a bigger family etc. instead she seems a very ambitious, career driven person, which is one of the top reasons why ppl don't have kids. (especially those who can get pregnant) - she's also easily annoyed, fiercely individualistic, and has little patience or tolerance for "whining". What about that seems like she'd relish having a constantly crying baby that is totally dependent on her and is going to hugely restrict her freedom? Making it different in another version of the character still counts as "changing" it to this effect. But if you want to restrict yourself to the rebuild version only, the way she generally says the word "brat" doesn't suggest much fondness for children. Plus if she wanted them, wouldn't she lament it? Just like she bemoans that she can't eat or sleep or get older. It would have been super easy to include a scene where she is sorrowfully looking at Hikari & her baby from the distance & shown to be feeling jealous. I've remember she mentions it when she is angry.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Konja7 » Thu Oct 10, 2024 6:53 pm Kendrix wrote: Actually she says it in the context of complaining how much she hates her period: "Why must I go through this every month just because I'm a girl? I never want children anyway!" Which I thought was a very relatable, lifelike & realistic statement and one of those moments where you notice that Anno really did his research on this stuff to avoid writing cliches (supposedly he reads novels by female authors to get the characters rights)

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by MsenjaKagami » Fri Oct 25, 2024 3:07 am Yknow something I occasionally wonder about, now that the rest of humanity is back, do the people in Village-3 move back to the city where presumably everyone else who came back (likely including their families) would be? Or would they stay in Village-3 since that's been their home for the last 14 years anyways. Like, I imagine living in the rural countryside out of necessity bc everywhere else is completely uninhabitable to humans probably wasn't all sunshine and rainbows, but plenty of the people in the village seemed happy with their lives there, and I imagine for some of the younger adults, theyve lived more of their lives there than they ever did in the city. And on top of that, would they even want to go back to what wouldve been a normal life in the city? Like living out in a village having to do agriculture to make food probably also isn't the easiest life, but also idk going back to the city to work some BS Nine to Five also doesn't really sound all that great either after living a life that, while objectively more difficult day to day, probably was/is more fulfilling. Not to mention how village life is a lot more communal where everyone's a lot more closer and cares about each other, whereas life in the city is a lot more atomized and lonely. Ive never lived in a village myself, but i imagine if I did, that'd be one thing I'd miss if I moved to the city

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by EvaFan304 » Mon Nov 11, 2024 8:12 am Yo, I think that Shinji and Mari started a relationship (they do not necessarily have children and stuff, just casual). Maybe Adam and Rei become a thing? I don't think that the characters are truly their version from rebuild (same way rebuild Shinji isn't NGE Shinji) as during his conversation with Rei, he explains that he wants her to find a world for her. Also, Adam and Rei don't like each other within NGE and Rebuild so it supports the pseudo-character theory. Only Mari and Shinji remember the Evas within the new world as they travelled there (which explains why Shinji has the choker). Dead people can come back (EoE Asuka comes back) so Misato and Kaji are alive and start a family. Toji continues his family with class rep. Kensuke seems to wanna be alone working so loneliness for him ig! PenPen is the Kaji/Katsuragi pet, Gendo works for the Government alongside Yui. He and Shinji have a good father-son bond. I think this bc it follows the manga ending (I believe the manga ending takes place when Shinji goes to college IN THAT UNIVERSE).

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Dec 31, 2024 5:56 pm Asunji_Yuko wrote: Eh......this feels like a child free adult projecting. Part of growing up is raising a family, usually your own kids. Otherwise you're pretty much a perpetual teenager/young adult, with few exceptions. Anno falls into this category. It's unfortunate because I feel like he'd get along with kids, perhaps expressing distaste at his son/daughter's taste in tokusatsu.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Thu Jan 02, 2025 1:18 am Axx°N N. wrote: Asunji_Yuko#944226 wrote: Eh......this feels like a child free adult projecting. Part of growing up is raising a family, usually your own kids. Otherwise you're pretty much a perpetual teenager/young adult, with few exceptions. Anno falls into this category. It's unfortunate because I feel like he'd get along with kids, perhaps expressing distaste at his son/daughter's taste in tokusatsu.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Jan 02, 2025 1:53 am Asunji_Yuko wrote: ...but not having kids (or adopting, what have you) pretty much guarantees that you remain a perpetual child.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Konja7 » Fri Jan 03, 2025 12:11 pm Honestly, the reason I don't want children is because I don't want the responsibility to raise a kid. I could see how that could be interpreted as "immature", but I don't really care. People shouldn't judge other for their deicision to not have kids. Axx°N N. wrote: Anyway, the main thing I think about during this kind of discussion is the fact that a huge, notable amount of philosophers, as in the great minds that shaped our society, were childless and many of them unmarried as well. Maybe if we stick to your definition, we can call them all children, but, like, who cares? They had more merit than you or I or, you know, actual children.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Thu Jan 09, 2025 7:18 pm Axx°N N. wrote: Asunji_Yuko#944252 wrote: ...but not having kids (or adopting, what have you) pretty much guarantees that you remain a perpetual child.

[]: Re: Life after Evangelion 3.0 + 1.0             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Jan 09, 2025 9:28 pm Asunji_Yuko wrote: While they're intellectually interesting, it's a bit of a stretch to say that they "shaped our society."

Making it different in another version of the character still counts as "changing" it to this effect. But if you want to restrict yourself to the rebuild version only, the way she generally says the word "brat" doesn't suggest much fondness for children. Plus if she wanted them, wouldn't she lament it? Just like she bemoans that she can't eat or sleep or get older. It would have been super easy to include a scene
